mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This mechanism operates continuously as long as FEDFUNDS remains in the easing regime, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.39% on 2008-11-01 indicates extreme easing
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 6.5% signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative forward returns. The mechanism activates when UNRATE crosses above the 6.5% threshold, indicating recession-level employment stress.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% on 2008-11-01 indicates elevated unemployment
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following UNRATE > 6.5% crossings are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 in triggered regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales decline measured by RSAFS combined with housing starts collapse measured by HOUST jointly signal demand destruction. When both series decline simultaneously, consumer spending and construction activity contract, producing negative equity returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking markets price recession.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 355655M and HOUST at 791K on 2008-11-01 show demand weakness
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of joint decline instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines signal supply-side contraction and reduced corporate profitability. When INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average, equity valuations compress due to lower expected earnings, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.12 on 2008-11-01 reflects manufacturing contraction
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO declines below 3-month MA are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment declines measured by PAYEMS combined with rising unemployment measured by UNRATE jointly signal labor market deterioration. When both series move adversely simultaneously, equity markets price in reduced consumer spending and recession risk, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136167K and UNRATE at 6.7% on 2008-11-01 show labor stress
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of joint adverse instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL remains stable below 3% while FEDFUNDS approaches zero, indicating the Fed has room for further easing without inflation concerns. This regime supports equity valuations through lower real discount rates, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 216.71 and FEDFUNDS at 0.39% on 2008-11-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in this regime are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST decline below 800K units, signaling residential construction collapse. This threshold indicates severe demand destruction in the housing sector, which cascades through construction employment and consumer wealth, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 791K on 2008-10-01 indicates housing crisis
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when HOUST < 800K are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP measured by GDPC1 contracts quarter-over-quarter, signaling economic recession. When GDPC1 growth turns negative, equity markets price in earnings contraction and recession duration, producing negative 60-day forward returns in the quarter following the GDP decline announcement.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11712.3B on 2008-07-01 reflects pre-crisis levels
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following negative GDPC1 quarters are positive in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window in recession periods.
